Title: Engineering rice with lower grain arsenic

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Root cortex- and internode/phloem-directed expression of OsABCC1 reduced arsenic accumulation in brown rice. 2. In combination with c-ECS and ScYCF1, OsABCC1 caused strong arsenic retention in roots, reduced xylem sap arsenic, and lowered shoot and grain arsenic by about 70%. 3. Ubiquitous expression was ineffective, demonstrating the importance of tissue-specific expression.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in rice; tissue-specific promoter comparison; subcellular localization by sucrose gradient/immunoblot; qRT-PCR; grain, tissue, and xylem sap arsenic profiling; stem arsenic feeding assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Engineered expression in root cortical cells, endodermis, and phloem cells of nodes/internodes under RCc3 promoter
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Endogenous OsABCC1 is likely already expressed in node tissues
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: As(III) exposure; DMA(V) specificity test; normal soil basal arsenic; grain-filling stage feeding assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vacuolar sequestration of inorganic arsenic in root cortex and internode/phloem tissues, reducing root-to-shoot and shoot-to-grain arsenic translocation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Human health relevance because reduced dietary arsenic lowers toxicity and cancer risk
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for engineering low-arsenic rice when expressed in translocation-relevant tissues rather than ubiquitously; should be stacked with thiol-boosting components and possibly extrusion traits for high-arsenic soils
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible arsenic hyperaccumulation and cell damage in roots/nongrain tissues under excess arsenic conditions; no major yield penalty under tested field conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Substrate specificity favors inorganic As(III), not DMA; Cd accumulation/translocation was unaffected

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized ScYCF1 was introduced as a vacuolar arsenic sequestration transporter in rice. 2. Together with OsABCC1 and c-ECS under RCc3 promoter control, ScYCF1 contributed to reduced arsenic translocation to shoots and grains. 3. Yeast assays and rice DMA treatments indicated that ScYCF1 does not recognize DMA as a major substrate.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic expression in rice; promoter comparison; arsenic profiling in tissues and grains; DMA sensitivity assay in yeast; stem arsenic feeding assay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Engineered expression in root cortical cells and phloem-associated internode/node tissues under RCc3 promoter
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: As(III) exposure; DMA(V) specificity test; normal soil basal arsenic; grain-filling stage arsenic feeding
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances vacuolar sequestration of inorganic arsenic and reduces arsenic movement into rice grains when stacked with OsABCC1 and c-ECS
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduction of grain arsenic improves food safety and human health
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful heterologous transporter for low-arsenic rice engineering in combination with OsABCC1 and thiol biosynthesis genes; tissue-specific deployment is critical
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible arsenic hyperaccumulation in roots/nongrain tissues under excessive arsenic exposure; no agronomic penalty under tested conditions
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Codon-optimized yeast gene; not supported as a DMA transporter

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lsi2 is described as the major arsenic efflux transporter localized to the proximal side of the endodermal layer that loads arsenic into the xylem. 2. Enhanced vacuolar sequestration by OsABCC1 and ScYCF1 is proposed to inhibit radial arsenic movement toward Lsi2. 3. Lsi2 itself was not experimentally manipulated in this study.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Background mechanistic inference only; not directly tested in this paper
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Plasma membrane of endodermis proximal side
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Background transporter mediating arsenic loading into xylem
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background gene only; included in model figure and discussion, not a core experimental gene

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lsi1 is mentioned in the model as contributing to arsenic uptake/translocation. 2. It was not manipulated or functionally tested in the Results section of this study.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Background gene only; not a core experimental subject

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Chelation and detoxification of arsenic, supporting vacuolar sequestration and reducing grain arsenic
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Root cortex vacuoles/cytosolic thiol pool in rice roots
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. c-ECS was coexpressed with OsABCC1 and ScYCF1 to increase nonprotein thiols (glutathione and phytochelatins) in the cytosol. 2. In the RCc3p-AEY combination, elevated thiol accumulation in root cortex cells accompanied reduced root-to-shoot and grain arsenic translocation. 3. c-ECS alone was not isolated as a sole determinant; its function was supportive to transporter-mediated arsenic sequestration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic coexpression in rice; qRT-PCR; metabolite-related thiol staining with monobromobimane; arsenic profiling in roots, shoots, and grains
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a stacking component with vacuolar arsenic transporters to enlarge thiol chelator pools and enhance arsenic detoxification; likely insufficient alone for lowering grain arsenic.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUBI promoter (constitutive); OsActin2 promoter (constitutive in UBIp-AE/AEY constructs)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutive transgene expression in engineered rice
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent formation of γ-glutamylcysteine from glutamate and cysteine, supplying precursor for glutathione and phytochelatin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports increased thiol accumulation and contributes to reduced arsenic translocation to shoots and grains when stacked with OsABCC1 and ScYCF1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: As(III) exposure in hydroponics; normal soil with basal arsenic; grain filling stage arsenic feeding
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: High arsenic sequestration in nongrain tissues may lead to hyperaccumulation and possible cell damage under excess arsenic fields, though no agronomic penalty under tested field conditions
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bacterial gene used for metabolic engineering; effect demonstrated only in stacked constructs, not as a standalone tested gene
